𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Four — Part Two

Divrei HaYamim Aleph

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH

Covenant History — The Reign of Dawid

Chapters 10 through 29

YHWH Gave Dawid Victory Wherever He Went — He Will Not Leave You or Forsake You — All Things Come From You and of Your Own Have We Given You — Dawid Died in a Good Old Age

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTERS 10 THROUGH 20 — THE COVENANT DAVIDIC KINGDOM

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 10 The Pelishtim Fought Against Yisra'EL — Sha'ul Fell on His Sword — Yavesh Gil'ad Took the Body — Creator Put Him to Death — He Sought Guidance From a Medium

1 Now the Pelishtim fought against Yisra'EL, and the men of Yisra'EL fled before the Pelishtim and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.

2 And the Pelishtim overtook Sha'ul and his sons, and the Pelishtim struck down Yonatan and Avinadav and Malkishua, the sons of Sha'ul.

3 The battle pressed hard against Sha'ul, and the archers found him, and he was wounded by the archers.

4 Then Sha'ul said to his armor-bearer, Draw your sword and thrust me through with it, lest these uncircumcised come and mistreat me. But his armor-bearer would not, for he feared greatly. Therefore Sha'ul took his own sword and fell upon it.

5 And when his armor-bearer saw that Sha'ul was dead, he also fell upon his sword and died.

6 Thus Sha'ul died. He and his three sons and all his house died together.

7 And when all the men of Yisra'EL who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Sha'ul and his sons were dead, they abandoned their cities and fled, and the Pelishtim came and lived in them.

8 The next day, when the Pelishtim came to strip the slain, they found Sha'ul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.

9 And they stripped him and took his head and his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Pelishtim to carry the good news to their idols and to the people.

10 And they put his armor in the house of their gods and fastened his head in the house of Dagon.

11 But when all Yavesh-Gil'ad heard all that the Pelishtim had done to Sha'ul,

12 All the valiant men arose and took away the body of Sha'ul and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Yavesh. And they buried their bones under the oak in Yavesh and fasted seven days.

13 So Sha'ul died for his breach of faith. He broke faith with YHWH in that he did not keep the command of YHWH, and also consulted a medium, seeking guidance.

14 He did not seek guidance from YHWH. Therefore YHWH put him to death and turned the kingdom over to Dawid the son of Yishai.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 10 is the covenant pivot from the covenant genealogical foundation to the covenant historical narrative: the covenant death of Sha'ul. The covenant compiler gives the covenant theological reason for Sha'ul's covenant death — verse 13-14 — with a precision not found in the parallel account of Shemu'el Aleph: he broke faith with YHWH, did not seek guidance from YHWH, and sought guidance from a medium. The covenant cause-and-effect is stated explicitly.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 13-14 — Sha'ul died for his breach of faith — he did not seek guidance from YHWH — therefore YHWH put him to death and turned the kingdom over to Dawid. The covenant theological explanation of the covenant historical event: Sha'ul's covenant failure is the covenant preparation for Dawid's covenant kingship. The covenant compiler sees the covenant death of Sha'ul as the covenant necessary covenant transition to the covenant Davidic covenant era.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 10 in full. The covenant brevity of the Sha'ul narrative in Divrei HaYamim compared to Shemu'el Aleph is covenant intentional: the covenant compiler's focus is the covenant Davidic covenant era. Sha'ul's covenant death is the covenant starting point, not the covenant story. The covenant compiler documents the covenant end of the covenant Sha'ulide era to frame the covenant beginning of the covenant Davidic era.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — he also consulted a medium, seeking guidance. The covenant sin of covenant divination — the same covenant sin condemned in Devarim 18:10-11 — is cited alongside covenant direct covenant disobedience as the twin causes of Sha'ul's covenant death. The covenant compiler draws the covenant explicit theological conclusion that the parallel account in Shemu'el Aleph leaves more covenant implicit: Sha'ul's covenant death was covenant judicial — YHWH put him to death.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 11 All Yisra'EL Gathered to Dawid — He Made a Covenant With Them — Dawid Took the Fortress of Tziyon — Yo'av Was Commander — Dawid's Mighty Men — The Three Who Broke Through — The Thirty

1 Then all Yisra'EL gathered together to Dawid at Khevron and said, Behold, we are your bone and flesh.

2 In times past, even when Sha'ul was king, it was you who led out and brought in Yisra'EL. And YHWH your Creator said to you, You shall be shepherd of my people Yisra'EL, and you shall be prince over my people Yisra'EL.

3 So all the elders of Yisra'EL came to the king at Khevron, and Dawid made a covenant with them at Khevron before YHWH. And they anointed Dawid king over Yisra'EL, according to the word of YHWH by Shemu'el.

4 And Dawid and all Yisra'EL went to Yerushalayim, that is Yevus, where the Yevusites were, the inhabitants of the land.

5 The inhabitants of Yevus said to Dawid, You will not come in here. Nevertheless, Dawid took the stronghold of Tziyon, that is, the city of Dawid.

6 Dawid said, Whoever strikes the Yevusites first shall be chief and commander. And Yo'av the son of Tzeruyah went up first, so he became chief.

7 And Dawid lived in the stronghold. Therefore it was called the city of Dawid.

8 And he built the city all around from the Millo in complete circuit, and Yo'av repaired the rest of the city.

9 And Dawid became greater and greater, for YHWH of hosts was with him.

10 Now these are the chiefs of Dawid's mighty warriors, who gave him strong support in his kingdom, together with all Yisra'EL, to make him king, according to the word of YHWH concerning Yisra'EL.

11 This is an account of Dawid's mighty warriors: Yashobeam, a Hakhmonite, was chief of the three. He wielded his spear against 300, whom he killed at one time.

12 And next to him among the three mighty warriors was El'azar the son of Dodo, the Ahohite.

13 He was with Dawid at Pas-Dammim when the Pelishtim were gathered there for battle. There was a plot of ground full of barley, and the men fled from the Pelishtim.

14 But he took his stand in the midst of the plot and defended it and killed the Pelishtim. And YHWH saved them by a great victory.

15 Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to Dawid at the cave of Adullam, when the army of Pelishtim was encamped in the Valley of Refa'im.

16 Dawid was then in the stronghold, and the garrison of the Pelishtim was then at Beit-Lekhem.

17 And Dawid said longingly, Oh that someone would give me water to drink from the well of Beit-Lekhem that is by the gate.

18 Then the three broke through the camp of the Pelishtim and drew water out of the well of Beit-Lekhem that was by the gate and took it and brought it to Dawid. But Dawid would not drink it. He poured it out to YHWH

19 And said, Far be it from me before my Creator that I should do this. Shall I drink the lifeblood of these men? For at the risk of their lives they brought it. Therefore he would not drink it. These things did the three mighty men.

20 Now Avishai, the brother of Yo'av, was chief of the thirty. And he wielded his spear against 300 and killed them and won a name beside the three.

21 He was the most renowned of the thirty and became their commander, but he did not attain to the three.

22 And Benayahu the son of Yehoyada was a valiant man of Kavtze'el, a doer of great deeds. He struck down two heroes of Moav. He also went down and struck down a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.

23 And he struck down an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits tall. The Egyptian had in his hand a spear like a weaver's beam, but Benayahu went down to him with a staff and snatched the spear out of the Egyptian's hand and killed him with his own spear.

24 These things did Benayahu the son of Yehoyada, and won a name beside the three mighty men.

25 He was renowned among the thirty, but he did not attain to the three. And Dawid set him over his bodyguard.

26 The mighty warriors of the armies: Asahel the brother of Yo'av, Elkhanah of Beit-Lekhem,

27 Shammot the Harorite, Kheletz the Pelonite,

28 Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Aviezer the Antothite,

29 Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,

30 Maharai the Netofathite, Kheled the son of Ba'anah the Netofathite,

31 Itai the son of Rivai of Giv'ah of the Binyaminites, Benayahu of Pir'aton,

32 Hurai of the brooks of Ga'ash, Avi'el the Arvathite,

33 Azmaveth the Bakharumite, Elyakhba the Sha'albonite,

34 The sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Yonatan the son of Shage the Hararite,

35 Akhiam the son of Sakar the Hararite, Elifal the son of Ur,

36 Khefer the Mekherathite, Akhiyah the Pelonite,

37 Khetzro the Karmelite, Na'arai the son of Ezbai,

38 Yo'el the brother of Natan, Mivkhar the son of Hagri,

39 Tzelek the Ammonite, Nakhrai the Berothite, the armor-bearer of Yo'av the son of Tzeruyah,

40 Ira the Yitrite, Garev the Yitrite,

41 Uriyah the Hittite, Zavad the son of Akhlai,

42 Adina the son of Shiza the Reubenite, a chief of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,

43 Hanan the son of Ma'akha, and Yoshafat the Mithnite,

44 Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Ye'iel the sons of Khotam the Aroerith,

45 Yedia'el the son of Shimri, and Yokha his brother, the Titzite,

46 Eliel the Makhavite, and Yerivai and Yoshavyah the sons of Elnaam, and Yitmah the Moabite,

47 Eliel, and Oved, and Ya'asi'el the Mezobaite.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 11 opens the covenant Dawid narrative by combining the covenant anointing at Khevron — verse 1-3 — the covenant taking of Yerushalayim — verse 4-9 — and the covenant catalog of Dawid's mighty men — verse 10-47. The covenant compiler's focus is covenant already on the covenant greatness of Dawid's covenant reign: YHWH of hosts was with him — verse 9.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 18-19 — the three brought water from Beit-Lekhem at the risk of their lives — Dawid would not drink it — he poured it out to YHWH and said, Far be it from me that I should do this — shall I drink the lifeblood of these men? The covenant character of the covenant king: Dawid's covenant response to the covenant act of covenant loyalty is covenant consecration of the water to YHWH rather than covenant personal covenant enjoyment of it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 11 in full. The list of Dawid's mighty men — verses 26-47 — is the covenant military foundation of the covenant Davidic kingdom: 37 named covenant warriors who formed the covenant inner circle of covenant martial excellence that enabled the covenant Davidic covenant conquests. The covenant inclusion of Uriyah the Hittite — verse 41 — within the list of covenant loyal covenant warriors is the covenant honest covenant record that includes the man whose covenant wife Dawid took.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — Dawid made a covenant with the elders of Yisra'EL at Khevron before YHWH. The covenant foundational political act of the covenant Davidic monarchy is a covenant formal covenant with the covenant people before YHWH. The covenant king is accountable to the covenant community through the covenant covenant relationship established before the covenant divine witness. The covenant monarchy of Yisra'EL is not merely covenant political but covenant covenantal.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 12 Those Who Came to Dawid at Tziklag — The Gadites Who Crossed the Yarden — The Men of Menasheh — All These Came to Dawid — A Great Army Like the Army of Creator

1 Now these are the men who came to Dawid at Tziklag, while he could not move about freely because of Sha'ul the son of Kish. And they were among the mighty men who helped him in war.

2 They were bowmen and could shoot arrows and sling stones with either the right or the left hand. They were Binyaminites, Sha'ul's kinsmen.

3 The chief was Akhiezer, then Yo'ash, both sons of Shemah of Giv'ah. And Yeziel and Pelet, the sons of Azmaveth, Berakha, Yehu of Anatot,

4 Yishma'yahu of Giv'on, a mighty man among the thirty and a leader over the thirty, Yirmeyahu, Yakhazi'el, Yokhanan, Yozavad of Gederah,

5 Eluzai, Yerimot, Be'alyah, Shemaryahu, Shefatyahu the Haruphite,

6 Elkanah, Yishshiyahu, Azarel, Yo'ezer, and Yashobeam, the Korahites,

7 And Yo'elah and Zevadyahu, the sons of Yeroham of Gedor.

8 From the Gadites there went over to Dawid at the stronghold in the wilderness mighty and experienced warriors, expert with shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions and who were swift as gazelles upon the mountains:

9 Ezer the chief, Ovadyahu second, Eliav third,

10 Mishmannah fourth, Yirmeyahu fifth,

11 Attai sixth, Eliel seventh,

12 Yokhanan eighth, Elzabad ninth,

13 Yirmeyahu tenth, Makhvannai eleventh.

14 These Gadites were officers of the army. The least was a match for a hundred men and the greatest for a thousand.

15 These are the men who crossed the Yarden in the first month, when it was overflowing all its banks, and put to flight all those in the valleys, to the east and to the west.

16 And some of the men of Binyamin and Yehudah came to the stronghold to Dawid.

17 Dawid went out to meet them and said to them, If you have come to me in friendship to help me, my heart will be joined to you, but if to betray me to my adversaries, although there is no wrong in my hands, then may the Creator of our fathers see and rebuke you.

18 Then the Spirit came upon Amasai, chief of the thirty, and he said, We are yours, O Dawid, and with you, O son of Yishai. Peace, peace to you, and peace to your helpers. For your Creator helps you. Then Dawid received them and made them officers of his troops.

19 Some of the men of Menasheh deserted to Dawid when he came with the Pelishtim for the battle against Sha'ul. Yet he did not help them, for the rulers of the Pelishtim took counsel and sent him away, saying, At peril of our heads he will desert to his master Sha'ul.

20 As he went to Tziklag, these men of Menasheh deserted to him: Adnakh, Yozavad, Yediael, Mikha'el, Yozavad, Elihu, and Tziltai, chiefs of thousands in Menasheh.

21 They helped Dawid against the band of raiders, for they were all mighty men of valor and were commanders in the army.

22 For from day to day men kept coming to Dawid to help him, until there was a great army, like an army of Creator.

23 These are the numbers of the divisions of the armed troops who came to Dawid in Khevron, to turn the kingdom of Sha'ul over to him, according to the word of YHWH.

24 The men of Yehudah bearing shield and spear were 6,800 armed troops.

25 Of the Shim'onites, mighty men of valor for war, 7,100.

26 Of the Levites, 4,600.

27 The prince Yehoyada, of the house of Aharon, and with him 3,700.

28 Tzadok, a young man mighty in valor, and twenty-two commanders from his father's house.

29 Of the Binyaminites, the kinsmen of Sha'ul, 3,000, of whom the majority had to that point kept their allegiance to the house of Sha'ul.

30 Of the Efrayimites, 20,800 mighty men of valor, famous men in their fathers' houses.

31 Of the half-tribe of Menasheh, 18,000, who were expressly named to come and make Dawid king.

32 Of Yissachar, men who had understanding of the times, to know what Yisra'EL ought to do, 200 chiefs, and all their kinsmen under their command.

33 Of Zevulun, 50,000 seasoned troops, equipped for battle with all the weapons of war, to help Dawid with singleness of purpose.

34 Of Naftali, 1,000 commanders with whom were 37,000 men armed with shield and spear.

35 Of the Danites, 28,600 men equipped for battle.

36 Of Asher, 40,000 seasoned troops ready for battle.

37 Of the Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Menasheh from beyond the Yarden, 120,000 men armed with all the weapons of war.

38 All these, men of war, arrayed in battle order, came to Khevron with full intent to make Dawid king over all Yisra'EL. Likewise, all the rest of Yisra'EL were of a single mind to make Dawid king.

39 And they were there with Dawid for three days, eating and drinking, for their brothers had made preparation for them.

40 And also their relatives, from as far as Yissachar, Zevulun, and Naftali, came bringing food on donkeys and on camels and on mules and on oxen, abundant provisions of flour, cakes of figs, clusters of raisins, and wine and oil and oxen and sheep, for there was joy in Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 12 documents the covenant building of the covenant Davidic coalition — from the covenant Binyaminite warriors who came to Tziklag through the covenant tribal contingents who came to Khevron. The covenant total covenant military assembly at Khevron is the covenant largest covenant gathering documented in the covenant Davidic narrative: every tribe represented, covenant single-minded — verse 38 — to make Dawid king.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 32 — the sons of Yissachar, men who had understanding of the times, to know what Yisra'EL ought to do. The covenant wisdom of Yissachar: the covenant ability to read the covenant moment and know the covenant appropriate covenant action. This covenant capacity for covenant discernment is presented as the covenant distinctive covenant contribution of the covenant tribe of Yissachar to the covenant national covenant life.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 22 — from day to day men kept coming to Dawid to help him, until there was a great army, like an army of Creator. The covenant growth of the covenant coalition is covenant attributed to divine covenant momentum: the covenant army that gathered around Dawid was covenant not merely a covenant political coalition but a covenant divine covenant mustering — like the army of Creator.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17-18 — Dawid's covenant speech to the warriors who came to him at the stronghold, and Amasai's covenant response under the covenant Spirit. The covenant Spirit that comes upon Amasai — the same covenant Spirit that empowered the covenant judges — produces a covenant loyalty declaration: we are yours, O Dawid. The covenant political covenant loyalty and the covenant Spirit-inspired covenant declaration are the same covenant event.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 13 Let Us Bring Back the Ark — They Carried the Ark on a New Cart — Uzza Put Out His Hand — The Anger of YHWH — The Ark Remained With Oved-Edom Three Months — YHWH Blessed His Household

1 Dawid consulted with the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, with every leader.

2 And Dawid said to all the assembly of Yisra'EL, If it seems good to you and from YHWH our Creator, let us send abroad to our brothers who remain in all the lands of Yisra'EL, as well as to the priests and Levites in the cities that have pasturelands, that they may be gathered to us.

3 Then let us bring again the ark of our Creator to us, for we did not seek it in the days of Sha'ul.

4 All the assembly agreed to do so, for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people.

5 So Dawid assembled all Yisra'EL from the Shihor of Egypt to Levo-Hamat, to bring the ark of Creator from Kiryat-Ye'arim.

6 And Dawid and all Yisra'EL went up to Ba'alah, that is, to Kiryat-Ye'arim that belongs to Yehudah, to bring up from there the ark of Creator, who is enthroned above the cherubim, which is called by his name.

7 And they carried the ark of Creator on a new cart, from the house of Avinadav, and Uzza and Akhyo were driving the cart.

8 And Dawid and all Yisra'EL were celebrating before Creator with all their might, with song and lyres and harps and tambourines and cymbals and trumpets.

9 And when they came to the threshing floor of Kidon, Uzza put out his hand to take hold of the ark, for the oxen stumbled.

10 And the anger of YHWH was kindled against Uzza, and he struck him down because he put out his hand to the ark, and he died there before Creator.

11 And Dawid was angry because YHWH had burst out against Uzza. And that place is called Peretz-Uzza to this day.

12 And Dawid was afraid of Creator that day, and he said, How can I bring the ark of Creator home to me?

13 So Dawid did not take the ark home into the city of Dawid, but took it aside to the house of Oved-Edom the Gittite.

14 And the ark of Creator remained with the household of Oved-Edom in his house three months. And YHWH blessed the household of Oved-Edom and all that he had.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. The covenant Ark narrative of Chapter 13 establishes the covenant theological principle that the covenant ark of YHWH cannot be treated carelessly even with covenant good intentions. The covenant new cart — a Philistine innovation — was not the covenant prescribed covenant means of carrying the covenant ark. The covenant result is the covenant death of Uzza and the covenant crisis of Dawid's covenant relationship with YHWH.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 3 — let us bring again the ark of our Creator to us, for we did not seek it in the days of Sha'ul. The covenant spiritual diagnosis of the Sha'ulide covenant era: the covenant ark was neglected. Dawid's first major covenant national initiative is the covenant restoration of the covenant ark to its covenant central place in the covenant national covenant life — the covenant religious covenant priority that Sha'ul failed to maintain.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 14 — the ark of Creator remained with the household of Oved-Edom three months. And YHWH blessed the household of Oved-Edom and all that he had. The covenant blessing of Oved-Edom's household is the covenant sign that the covenant ark is not merely a covenant dangerous object but a covenant source of covenant blessing for those who receive it with covenant reverence. The covenant blessing is the covenant confirmation that the covenant ark can be brought to Dawid.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — Dawid was afraid of Creator that day. The covenant fear of YHWH that Dawid experiences after Uzza's death is not covenant terror but covenant awe — the covenant proper covenant response to the covenant encounter with the covenant holiness of YHWH that exceeds covenant human control. The covenant ark is not a covenant talisman that the covenant community manages — it is the covenant dwelling of YHWH's covenant presence that operates according to YHWH's covenant principles.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 14 Khiram Sent Messengers to Dawid — The Sons Born in Yerushalayim — Dawid Inquired of Creator — You Shall Not Go Up After Them — When You Hear the Sound in the Tops of the Trees

1 And Khiram king of Tzor sent messengers to Dawid, and cedar trees, also masons and carpenters to build a house for him.

2 And Dawid knew that YHWH had established him as king over Yisra'EL, and that his kingdom was highly exalted for the sake of his people Yisra'EL.

3 And Dawid took more wives in Yerushalayim, and Dawid fathered more sons and daughters.

4 These are the names of the children born to him in Yerushalayim: Shammua, Shovav, Natan, Shelomoh,

5 Yivkhar, Elishua, Elpelet,

6 Nogah, Nefeg, Yafia,

7 Elishama, Ba'alyada, and Elifelet.

8 When the Pelishtim heard that Dawid had been anointed king over all Yisra'EL, all the Pelishtim went up to seek Dawid. And Dawid heard of it and went out against them.

9 Now the Pelishtim had come and made a raid in the Valley of Refa'im.

10 And Dawid inquired of Creator, Shall I go up against the Pelishtim? Will you give them into my hand? And YHWH said to him, Go up, and I will give them into your hand.

11 And he went up to Ba'al-Peratzim, and Dawid struck them down there. And Dawid said, Creator has broken through my enemies by my hand, like a bursting flood. Therefore the name of that place is called Ba'al-Peratzim.

12 And they left their gods there, and Dawid gave command, and they were burned.

13 And the Pelishtim yet again made a raid in the valley.

14 And Dawid again inquired of Creator, and Creator said to him, You shall not go up after them. Go around and come against them opposite the balsam trees.

15 And when you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, then go out to battle, for Creator has gone out before you to strike down the army of the Pelishtim.

16 And Dawid did as Creator commanded him, and they struck down the Pelishtim from Givon to Gezer.

17 And the fame of Dawid went out into all lands, and YHWH brought the fear of him upon all nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 14 establishes the covenant pattern of covenant Davidic kingship that the covenant compiler celebrates: Dawid inquires of YHWH before covenant military covenant action — verse 10, 14 — and YHWH provides covenant specific covenant guidance. The covenant second battle includes a covenant supernatural covenant sign — the sound of marching in the treetops — indicating that YHWH himself has gone out before Dawid.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 2 — Dawid knew that YHWH had established him as king over Yisra'EL, and that his kingdom was highly exalted for the sake of his people Yisra'EL. The covenant self-awareness of Dawid: the covenant kingship is not for his own covenant benefit but for the sake of the covenant people. The covenant covenant king understands his covenant position as covenant service, not covenant privilege.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 12 — they left their gods there, and Dawid gave command, and they were burned. The covenant contrast with the parallel account in Shemu'el Bet 5:21 — which says they were carried away. The covenant compiler of Divrei HaYamim changes carried away to burned — the covenant theologically appropriate covenant treatment of covenant idols according to Devarim 7:25.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant pattern of covenant inquiry — verse 10 and 14 — is the covenant distinctive covenant characteristic of the covenant Dawid presented in Divrei HaYamim: a covenant king who does not trust his own covenant military judgment but consistently seeks covenant divine covenant direction. The covenant second battle receives a covenant different covenant answer from the covenant first — go around, not straight up — demonstrating that YHWH's covenant guidance is covenant contextually specific, not a covenant generic formula.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 15 Dawid Prepared a Place for the Ark — No One But the Levites May Carry the Ark — Dawid Called the Priests and Levites — They Carried the Ark With the Poles — Mikhal Despised Him in Her Heart

1 Dawid built houses for himself in the city of Dawid. And he prepared a place for the ark of Creator and pitched a tent for it.

2 Then Dawid said, No one but the Levites may carry the ark of Creator, for YHWH chose them to carry the ark of Creator and to minister to him forever.

3 And Dawid assembled all Yisra'EL at Yerushalayim to bring up the ark of YHWH to its place, which he had prepared for it.

4 And Dawid gathered together the sons of Aharon and the Levites:

5 Of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, with 120 of his brothers.

6 Of the sons of Merari, Asayah the chief, with 220 of his brothers.

7 Of the sons of Gershom, Yo'el the chief, with 130 of his brothers.

8 Of the sons of Elitzafan, Shemayah the chief, with 200 of his brothers.

9 Of the sons of Khevron, Eliel the chief, with 80 of his brothers.

10 Of the sons of Uzzi'el, Amminadav the chief, with 112 of his brothers.

11 Dawid summoned the priests Tzadok and Evyatar, and the Levites Uriel, Asayah, Yo'el, Shemayah, Eliel, and Amminadav,

12 And said to them, You are the heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites. Consecrate yourselves, you and your brothers, so that you may bring up the ark of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, to the place that I have prepared for it.

13 Because you did not carry it the first time, YHWH our Creator broke out against us, because we did not seek him according to the rule.

14 So the priests and the Levites consecrated themselves to bring up the ark of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

15 And the Levites carried the ark of Creator on their shoulders with the poles, as Mosheh had commanded according to the word of YHWH.

16 Dawid also commanded the chiefs of the Levites to appoint their brothers as the singers who should play loudly on musical instruments, on harps and lyres and cymbals, to raise sounds of joy.

17 So the Levites appointed Heman the son of Yo'el, and of his brothers Asaf the son of Berekhyahu, and of the sons of Merari, their brothers, Eitan the son of Kushayahu.

18 And with them their brothers of the second order: Zekhariyah, Ya'azi'el, Shemiramot, Yekhiel, Unni, Eli'av, Benayahu, Ma'aseiyahu, Mattityahu, Elifelehu, and Mikneyahu, and Oved-Edom and Ye'iel, the gatekeepers.

19 The singers, Heman, Asaf, and Eitan, were to sound bronze cymbals.

20 Zekhariyah, Azi'el, Shemiramot, Yekhiel, Unni, Eli'av, Ma'aseiyahu, and Benayahu were to play harps according to Alamot.

21 But Mattityahu, Elifelehu, Mikneyahu, Oved-Edom, Ye'iel, and Azazyahu were to lead with lyres according to the Sheminit.

22 Kenanyahu, the chief of the Levites, was in charge of the music, for he was skilled in it.

23 Berekhyahu and Elkanah were to be gatekeepers for the ark.

24 Shevanyahu, Yoshafat, Netane'el, Amasai, Zekhariyahu, Benayahu, and Eli'ezer, the priests, were to blow the trumpets before the ark of Creator. Oved-Edom and Yekhiyahu were also to be gatekeepers for the ark.

25 So Dawid and the elders of Yisra'EL and the commanders of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of YHWH from the house of Oved-Edom with rejoicing.

26 And because Creator helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.

27 Dawid was clothed with a robe of fine linen, as also were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, and the singers and Kenanyahu the leader of the music of the singers. And Dawid wore a linen ephod.

28 So all Yisra'EL brought up the ark of the covenant of YHWH with shouting, to the sound of the horn, trumpets, and cymbals, and made loud music on harps and lyres.

29 And as the ark of the covenant of YHWH came to the city of Dawid, Mikhal the daughter of Sha'ul looked out of the window and saw King Dawid dancing and celebrating, and she despised him in her heart.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 15 is the covenant correction of the covenant mistake of Chapter 13: Dawid learns from the covenant death of Uzza that the covenant ark must be carried according to covenant Mosaic covenant instruction — by Levites with poles, not on a new cart. The covenant second ark-carrying is marked by covenant careful covenant obedience to the covenant Torah requirement.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 13 — because you did not carry it the first time, YHWH our Creator broke out against us, because we did not seek him according to the rule. Dawid's covenant self-assessment: the covenant failure at Peretz-Uzza was the covenant community's covenant failure to seek YHWH according to the covenant rule. The covenant correction requires covenant seeking YHWH's covenant way, not merely covenant good intention.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 15 in full. Verse 16 — Dawid commanded the chiefs of the Levites to appoint their brothers as the singers who should play loudly on musical instruments, on harps and lyres and cymbals, to raise sounds of joy. The covenant musical covenant institution of the covenant Levitical singers is established by Dawid's covenant command at this covenant moment — the covenant ark's entry into Yerushalayim is the covenant founding moment of the covenant Levitical music covenant tradition.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 29 — Mikhal the daughter of Sha'ul looked out of the window and saw King Dawid dancing and celebrating, and she despised him in her heart. The covenant tension between the covenant old order — Sha'ul's covenant daughter — and the covenant new order — Dawid dancing before YHWH — is captured in this covenant moment. The covenant king's covenant uninhibited covenant worship is the covenant occasion of the covenant final rupture with the covenant Sha'ulide covenant house.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 16 The Ark Was Brought and Set in the Tent — Offerings Were Made — Dawid Distributed Food — The Song of Praise — O Give Thanks to YHWH — He Is YHWH Our Creator — Asaf Left Before the Ark

1 And they brought in the ark of Creator and set it inside the tent that Dawid had pitched for it, and they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before Creator.

2 And when Dawid had finished offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of YHWH

3 And distributed to all Yisra'EL, both men and women, to each a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins.

4 Then he appointed some of the Levites as ministers before the ark of YHWH, to invoke, to thank, and to praise YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

5 Asaf was the chief, and second to him Zekhariah, then Ye'iel, Shemiramot, Yekhiel, Mattityahu, Eli'av, Benayahu, Oved-Edom, and Ye'iel, who were to play harps and lyres. Asaf was to sound the cymbals.

6 And Benayahu and Yakhazi'el the priests were to blow trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of Creator.

7 Then on that day Dawid first appointed that thanksgiving be sung to YHWH by Asaf and his brothers.

9 Sing to him, sing praises to him, tell of all his wondrous works.

20 Wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,

28 Ascribe to YHWH, O clans of the peoples, ascribe to YHWH glory and strength.

29 Ascribe to YHWH the glory due his name. Bring an offering and come before him. Worship YHWH in the splendor of holiness.

31 Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice, and let them say among the nations, YHWH is king.

36 Blessed be YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, from everlasting to everlasting. Then all the people said, Amen, and praised YHWH.

37 So Dawid left Asaf and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of YHWH to minister regularly before the ark as each day required,

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant psalm of Chapter 16 — verses 8-36 — is the covenant Davidic song of covenant thanksgiving that combines material from Tehillim 105, 96, and 106. The covenant placement of this covenant praise poetry within the covenant historical narrative of the ark's arrival is the covenant compiler's theological statement: covenant historical events and covenant liturgical responses belong together. The covenant history produces covenant praise; the covenant praise interprets the covenant history.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 15 — remember his covenant forever, the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations. The covenant permanence of the covenant: not merely the covenant current generation's covenant relationship with YHWH but the covenant commitment that spans a thousand covenant generations. The covenant community that gathers around the covenant ark is covenantally connected to Avraham, Yitzkhak, and Yaakov through the covenant enduring covenant word.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 16 in full. Verse 34 — oh give thanks to YHWH, for he is good. For his steadfast love endures forever. The covenant central covenant refrain of the covenant praise tradition — found in multiple Tehillim — is here placed in the covenant mouth of Asaf at the covenant founding moment of the covenant Levitical music tradition. This covenant refrain is the covenant theological center of the covenant community's covenant worship.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 39-40 — Tzadok the priest was left at Givon to offer burnt offerings regularly morning and evening according to the Torah. The covenant dual worship arrangement: the covenant ark is in Yerushalayim with Asaf leading covenant music, while the covenant Mosaic covenant altar is still at Givon with Tzadok offering covenant sacrifices. The covenant Solomonic temple will unify these two covenant worship centers — but for now, the covenant community worships at two covenant locations simultaneously.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 17 Dawid Wanted to Build the House — You Shall Not Build the House — I Took You From the Pasture — I Will Raise Up Your Offspring After You — His Throne Shall Be Established Forever — Your House and Your Kingdom Shall Be Made Sure Forever

1 Now when Dawid lived in his house, Dawid said to Natan the prophet, Behold, I dwell in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of YHWH is under a tent.

2 And Natan said to Dawid, Do all that is in your heart, for Creator is with you.

3 But that same night the word of Creator came to Natan,

4 Go and tell my servant Dawid, Thus says YHWH: You shall not build me a house to dwell in.

5 For I have not lived in a house since the day I led up Yisra'EL to this day, but I have gone from tent to tent and from dwelling to dwelling.

6 In all places where I have moved with all Yisra'EL, did I speak a word with any of the judges of Yisra'EL, whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, Why have you not built me a house of cedar?

7 Now therefore thus shall you say to my servant Dawid, Thus says YHWH of hosts, I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep, to be leader over my people Yisra'EL.

8 And I have been with you wherever you went and have cut off all your enemies from before you. And I will make for you a name, like the name of the great ones of the earth.

9 And I will appoint a place for my people Yisra'EL and will plant them, that they may dwell in their own place and be disturbed no more. And violent men shall waste them no more, as formerly,

10 From the time that I appointed judges over my people Yisra'EL. And I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover, I declare to you that YHWH will build you a house.

11 When your days are fulfilled to walk with your fathers, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.

12 He shall build a house for me, and I will establish his throne forever.

13 I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son. I will not take my steadfast love from him, as I took it from him who was before you,

14 But I will confirm him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be established forever.

15 In accordance with all these words, and in accordance with all this vision, Natan spoke to Dawid.

16 Then King Dawid went in and sat before YHWH and said, Who am I, O YHWH Creator, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?

17 And this was a small thing in your eyes, O Creator. You have also spoken of your servant's house for a great while to come, and have shown me future generations, O YHWH Creator.

18 And what more can Dawid say to you for honoring your servant? For you know your servant.

19 For your servant's sake, O YHWH, and according to your own heart, you have done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.

20 There is none like you, O YHWH, and there is no Creator besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

21 And who is like your people Yisra'EL, the one nation on earth whom Creator went to redeem to be his people, making for yourself a name for great and awesome things, in driving out nations before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt?

22 And you made your people Yisra'EL to be your people forever, and you, O YHWH, became their Creator.

23 And now, O YHWH, let the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house be established forever, and do as you have spoken.

24 And let your name be established and magnified forever, saying, YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, is Yisra'EL's Creator, and the house of your servant Dawid will be established before you.

25 For you, my Creator, have revealed to your servant that you will build a house for him. Therefore your servant has found courage to pray before you.

26 And now, O YHWH, you are Creator, and you have promised this good thing to your servant.

27 Now you have been pleased to bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you, for it is you, O YHWH, who have blessed, and it is blessed forever.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The covenant Davidic covenant of Chapter 17 — the covenant promise that YHWH will build Dawid a covenant house — is the covenant theological center of the entire Divrei HaYamim narrative. The covenant oracle through Natan establishes the covenant Davidic dynasty on a covenant eternal covenant foundation: not merely covenant political succession but covenant divine covenant commitment — his throne shall be established forever — verse 14.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verse 13 — I will not take my steadfast love from him as I took it from him who was before you. The covenant contrast between Sha'ul and the covenant Davidic successor: Sha'ul experienced the covenant withdrawal of YHWH's steadfast love because of covenant faithlessness. The covenant Davidic dynasty is covenant promised that the covenant steadfast love will not be withdrawn from it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 17 in full. Verse 16 — King Dawid went in and sat before YHWH and said, Who am I, O YHWH Creator, and what is my house? The covenant posture of Dawid's covenant prayer: sitting before YHWH — not kneeling or prostrating — in the covenant intimacy of covenant dialogue. The covenant prayer of Chapter 17 is the covenant model of covenant humble covenant reception of covenant extraordinary covenant promise.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — you shall not build me a house to dwell in. The covenant denial of Dawid's covenant desire to build the covenant temple is presented in Divrei HaYamim without the covenant reason given in Shemu'el Bet 7 — there the reason is implicit. Divrei HaYamim will give the covenant reason explicitly later: Dawid is a covenant man of war and has shed blood — Chapter 22:8. The covenant compiler preserves Dawid's covenant dignity by not emphasizing the covenant limitation here but allowing the covenant reason to emerge elsewhere.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 18 Dawid Defeated the Pelishtim — He Subdued Moav — He Struck Down Hadadezer — He Put Garrisons in Aram — YHWH Gave Dawid Victory Wherever He Went — Dawid's Officials

1 After this Dawid defeated the Pelishtim and subdued them, and he took Gat and its villages out of the hand of the Pelishtim.

2 And he defeated Moav, and the Moabites became servants to Dawid and brought tribute.

3 Dawid also defeated Hadadezer king of Tzovar-Tzovah toward Hamat, as he went to set up his monument at the river Euphrates.

4 And Dawid took from him 1,000 chariots, 7,000 horsemen, and 20,000 foot soldiers. And Dawid hamstrung all the chariot horses, but left enough for 100 chariots.

5 And when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Tzovar-Tzovah, Dawid struck down 22,000 men of the Syrians.

6 Then Dawid put garrisons in Syria of Damascus, and the Syrians became servants to Dawid and brought tribute. And YHWH gave Dawid victory wherever he went.

7 And Dawid took the shields of gold that were carried by the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Yerushalayim.

8 And from Tivchat and from Kun, cities of Hadadezer, Dawid took a large amount of bronze. With it Shelomoh made the bronze sea and the pillars and the vessels of bronze.

9 When To'u king of Hamat heard that Dawid had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer king of Tzovar-Tzovah,

10 He sent his son Hadoram to King Dawid to greet him and to congratulate him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him — for Hadadezer had often been at war with To'u — and he sent all sorts of articles of gold, of silver, and of bronze.

11 These also King Dawid dedicated to YHWH, together with the silver and gold that he had carried off from all the nations, from Edom, Moav, the Ammonites, the Pelishtim, and Amalek.

12 And Avishai the son of Tzeruyah defeated 18,000 Edomites in the Valley of Salt.

13 And he put garrisons in Edom, and all the Edomites became servants to Dawid. And YHWH gave Dawid victory wherever he went.

14 So Dawid reigned over all Yisra'EL, and he administered justice and equity to all his people.

15 Yo'av the son of Tzeruyah was over the army. Yehoshafat the son of Akhilud was recorder.

16 Tzadok the son of Akhituv and Avimelekh the son of Evyatar were priests. Shavsha was secretary.

17 Benayahu the son of Yehoyada was over the Kerethites and the Pelethites. And Dawid's sons were the chief officials in the service of the king.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 18 is the covenant summary of Dawid's covenant military expansions — defeating the Pelishtim, Moav, Aram, Edom. The covenant refrain — YHWH gave Dawid victory wherever he went — verse 6, 13 — is the covenant theological explanation of the covenant military expansion: the covenant victories are covenant attributed to YHWH's covenant action through Dawid, not to Dawid's covenant independent covenant power.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 14 — Dawid reigned over all Yisra'EL, and he administered justice and equity to all his people. The covenant summary of Dawid's covenant governance is covenant judicial: the covenant king's primary covenant domestic covenant responsibility is covenant justice and covenant equity. The covenant military covenant victory is the covenant means of covenant security; covenant justice is the covenant content of covenant kingship.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 11 — Dawid dedicated to YHWH all the materials taken in conquest: the gold, silver, and bronze from all the nations. The covenant dedication of covenant military spoils to YHWH is the covenant pattern of covenant Davidic kingship: the covenant wealth generated by covenant conquest is covenant consecrated to covenant divine covenant service — specifically to the covenant future temple building that Shelomoh will complete.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant list of Dawid's officials — verse 14-17 — establishes the covenant administrative covenant structure of the covenant Davidic state: a commander of the army, a recorder, two priests, a secretary, and a commander of the bodyguard. The covenant administrative covenant organization of the covenant kingdom mirrors covenant efficient covenant governance while maintaining covenant religious covenant dual-leadership through two covenant priests — Tzadok and Avimelekh.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 19 Khanum the King of the Ammonites Died — Dawid Sent Messengers to Comfort Him — Khanum Humiliated Dawid's Servants — Yo'av Set the Battle in Array — Be Strong for Our People — YHWH Do What Seems Good to Him

1 After this Nakhash the king of the Ammonites died, and his son Khanum reigned in his place.

2 And Dawid said, I will deal kindly with Khanum the son of Nakhash, for his father dealt kindly with me. So Dawid sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. And Dawid's servants came to the land of the Ammonites to Khanum to comfort him.

3 But the princes of the Ammonites said to Khanum, Do you think, because Dawid has sent comforters to you, that he is honoring your father? Have not his servants come to you to search and to overthrow and to spy out the land?

4 So Khanum took Dawid's servants and shaved them and cut off their garments in the middle, at their hips, and sent them away.

5 And Dawid was told concerning the men. And he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, Remain at Yericho until your beards have grown, and then return.

6 When the Ammonites saw that they had become a stench to Dawid, Khanum and the Ammonites sent 1,000 talents of silver to hire chariots and horsemen from Mesopotamia, from Aram-Ma'akhah, and from Tzovar-Tzovah.

7 They hired 32,000 chariots and the king of Ma'akhah with his army, who came and encamped before Medeva. And the Ammonites were mustered from their cities and came to battle.

8 When Dawid heard of it, he sent Yo'av and all the army of the mighty men.

9 And the Ammonites came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the city, and the kings who had come were by themselves in the open country.

10 When Yo'av saw that the battle was set against him both in front and in the rear, he chose some of the best men of Yisra'EL and arrayed them against the Syrians.

11 The rest of his men he put in the charge of Avishai his brother, and they were arrayed against the Ammonites.

12 And he said, If the Syrians are too strong for me, then you shall help me, but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will help you.

13 Be strong, and let us use our strength for our people and for the cities of our Creator, and may YHWH do what seems good to him.

14 So Yo'av and the people who were with him drew near before the Syrians for battle, and they fled before him.

15 And when the Ammonites saw that the Syrians fled, they also fled before Avishai his brother and entered the city. Then Yo'av came to Yerushalayim.

16 But when the Syrians saw that they had been defeated by Yisra'EL, they sent messengers and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the Euphrates, with Shofakh the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.

17 And when it was told to Dawid, he gathered all Yisra'EL together and crossed the Yarden and came to them and drew up his forces against them. And Dawid set the battle in array against the Syrians, and they fought with him.

18 And the Syrians fled before Yisra'EL, and Dawid killed of the Syrians the men of 7,000 chariots and 40,000 foot soldiers, and put to death also Shofakh the commander of their army.

19 And when the servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Yisra'EL, they made peace with Dawid and became subject to him. So the Syrians were not willing to save the Ammonites anymore.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. The covenant Ammonite war of Chapter 19 begins with a covenant act of covenant covenant-keeping: Dawid's covenant attempt to extend covenant kindness to Khanum in honor of his dead father's covenant kindness. The covenant misreading of Dawid's covenant gesture — the Ammonites interpret covenant kindness as covenant espionage — triggers the covenant conflict. Covenant good faith is misread as covenant bad faith.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 13 — be strong, and let us use our strength for our people and for the cities of our Creator, and may YHWH do what seems good to him. Yo'av's covenant battle speech is the covenant model of covenant humble covenant courage: fight with all covenant strength — and then release the outcome to YHWH's covenant sovereign covenant will. Covenant human effort and covenant divine sovereignty are held together without covenant contradiction.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 19 in full. The covenant diplomatic covenant failure of verses 1-5 — Dawid's covenant messengers of covenant comfort are humiliated by the Ammonites — is the covenant occasion for the covenant military covenant expansion of Chapter 19-20. The covenant offense against Dawid's covenant ambassadors — shaving and cutting their garments — is a covenant grave covenant diplomatic covenant insult that requires covenant military covenant response.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — may YHWH do what seems good to him. The covenant theology of covenant military covenant engagement: Yo'av fights with covenant human strength and entrusts the covenant outcome to YHWH's covenant sovereign covenant will. This is the covenant opposite of covenant presumption — the covenant community acts within its covenant capacity and trusts YHWH for the covenant result. The covenant theology of covenant humble covenant action before covenant divine covenant sovereignty is the covenant central covenant lesson of the entire covenant Davidic military covenant narrative.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 20 Yo'av Struck Rabbah — Dawid Took the Crown From Their King — Yisra'EL Fought the Pelishtim — Descendants of the Giants Were Struck Down

1 In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, Yo'av led out the army and ravaged the country of the Ammonites and came and besieged Rabbah. But Dawid remained at Yerushalayim. And Yo'av struck Rabbah and overthrew it.

2 And Dawid took the crown of their king from his head. He found that it weighed a talent of gold, and in it was a precious stone. And it was placed on Dawid's head. And he brought out the spoil of the city, a very great amount.

3 And he brought out the people who were in it and set them to labor with saws and iron picks and axes. And thus Dawid did to all the cities of the Ammonites. Then Dawid and all the people returned to Yerushalayim.

4 And after this there arose war with the Pelishtim at Gezer. Then Sibbecai the Hushathite struck down Sippai, who was one of the descendants of the giants, and the Pelishtim were subdued.

5 And there was again war with the Pelishtim, and Elkhanah the son of Yair struck down Lakhmi the brother of Golyat the Gittite, the shaft of whose spear was like a weaver's beam.

6 And there was again war at Gat, where there was a man of great stature, who had six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot, twenty-four in number, and he also was descended from the giants.

7 And when he taunted Yisra'EL, Yonatan the son of Shim'a, Dawid's brother, struck him down.

8 These were descended from the giants in Gat, and they fell by the hand of Dawid and by the hand of his servants.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 20 is the covenant compiler's abbreviated account of the Ammonite and Philistine wars. The covenant compiler omits the covenant Batsheva incident — 2 Shemu'el 11-12 — entirely. This covenant omission is the covenant most significant covenant editorial decision in Divrei HaYamim: the covenant compiler preserves Dawid's covenant dignity for the covenant post-exilic community by focusing on the covenant Davidic covenant ideal rather than the covenant Davidic covenant failure.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full. Verse 2 — Dawid took the crown of their king and placed it on his own head. The covenant symbol of covenant Ammonite submission: the covenant royal crown of the covenant enemy king becomes the covenant crown of the covenant Davidic king. The covenant transfer of covenant royal covenant authority is the covenant sign of the covenant completion of the covenant Ammonite war.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 20 in full. The brevity of Chapter 20 — only 8 verses — covering the covenant completion of wars that occupy multiple covenant chapters in Shemu'el Bet is the covenant measure of the covenant compiler's covenant editorial selectivity. The covenant compiler includes the covenant outcomes — Rabbah subdued, Pelishtim defeated, giants killed — without the covenant context that includes the covenant Davidic covenant moral failures.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — Dawid remained at Yerushalayim. The covenant phrase that in Shemu'el Bet 11 introduces the covenant Batsheva covenant narrative is here left as a bare covenant fact without the covenant consequence narrative. The covenant compiler of Divrei HaYamim knows the covenant story — the covenant audience knows the covenant story — but the covenant compiler's covenant purpose is not the covenant full covenant biography of Dawid but the covenant portrait of the covenant Davidic covenant ideal that the covenant post-exilic community needs to be inspired by.

CHAPTERS 21 THROUGH 29 — THE COVENANT PREPARATION FOR THE TEMPLE AND THE DEATH OF DAWID

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 21 Satan Incited Dawid to Number Yisra'EL — Yo'av Reported the Number — YHWH Was Displeased — The Angel of YHWH — Dawid Bought the Threshing Floor — This Is the House of YHWH Creator

1 Then Satan stood against Yisra'EL and incited Dawid to number Yisra'EL.

2 So Dawid said to Yo'av and the commanders of the people, Go, number Yisra'EL, from Beersheba to Dan, and bring me a report, that I may know their number.

3 But Yo'av said, May YHWH add to his people a hundred times as many as they are. Are they not, my lord the king, all of them my lord's servants? Why then should my lord require this? Why should it be a cause of guilt for Yisra'EL?

4 But the king's word prevailed against Yo'av. So Yo'av departed and went throughout all Yisra'EL and came back to Yerushalayim.

5 And Yo'av gave the sum of the numbering of the people to Dawid. In all Yisra'EL there were 1,100,000 men who drew the sword, and in Yehudah 470,000 who drew the sword.

6 But he did not include Levi and Binyamin in the numbering, for the king's command was abhorrent to Yo'av.

7 But Creator was displeased with this thing, and he struck Yisra'EL.

8 And Dawid said to Creator, I have sinned greatly in that I have done this thing. But now, please take away the iniquity of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.

9 And YHWH spoke to Gad, Dawid's seer, saying,

10 Go and say to Dawid, Thus says YHWH, Three things I offer you. Choose one of them, that I may do it to you.

11 So Gad came to Dawid and said to him, Thus says YHWH, Choose what you will:

12 Either three years of famine, or three months of devastation by your foes while the sword of your enemies overtakes you, or else three days of the sword of YHWH, pestilence on the land, with the angel of YHWH destroying throughout all the territory of Yisra'EL. Now decide what answer I shall return to him who sent me.

13 Then Dawid said to Gad, I am in great distress. Let me fall into the hand of YHWH, for his mercy is very great, but do not let me fall into the hand of man.

14 So YHWH sent a pestilence on Yisra'EL, and 70,000 men of Yisra'EL fell.

15 And Creator sent the angel to Yerushalayim to destroy it, but as he was about to destroy it, YHWH saw, and he relented from the calamity. And he said to the angel who was destroying, It is enough. Now stay your hand. And the angel of YHWH was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Yevusite.

16 And Dawid lifted his eyes and saw the angel of YHWH standing between earth and heaven, and in his hand a drawn sword stretched out over Yerushalayim. Then Dawid and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell upon their faces.

17 And Dawid said to Creator, Was it not I who gave command to number the people? It is I who have sinned and done great evil. But these sheep, what have they done? Please let your hand, O YHWH my Creator, be against me and against my father's house. But do not let the plague be on your people.

18 Now the angel of YHWH had commanded Gad to say to Dawid that Dawid should go up and raise an altar to YHWH on the threshing floor of Ornan the Yevusite.

19 So Dawid went up at Gad's word, which he had spoken in the name of YHWH.

20 Now Ornan had turned and saw the angel, and his four sons who were with him hid themselves. As Ornan was threshing wheat,

21 Dawid came to Ornan. And when Ornan looked and saw Dawid, he went out from the threshing floor and paid homage to Dawid with his face to the ground.

22 And Dawid said to Ornan, Give me the site of the threshing floor so that I may build on it an altar to YHWH — give it to me at its full price — that the plague may be averted from the people.

23 Then Ornan said to Dawid, Take it, and let my lord the king do what seems good to him. See, I give the oxen for burnt offerings and the threshing sledges for the wood and the wheat for a grain offering. I give it all.

24 But King Dawid said to Ornan, No, but I will buy them for the full price. I will not take for YHWH what is yours, nor offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.

25 So Dawid paid Ornan 600 shekels of gold by weight for the site.

26 And Dawid built there an altar to YHWH and presented burnt offerings and peace offerings and called on YHWH, and YHWH answered him with fire from heaven upon the altar of burnt offering.

27 Then YHWH commanded the angel, and he put his sword back into its sheath.

28 At that time, when Dawid saw that YHWH had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Yevusite, he sacrificed there.

29 For the tabernacle of YHWH, which Mosheh had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt offering were at that time in the high place at Givon.

30 But Dawid could not go before it to inquire of Creator, for he was afraid of the sword of the angel of YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 21 is the covenant theological pivot of the Dawid narrative: the covenant census, the covenant plague, the covenant purchase of Ornan's threshing floor, and the covenant altar. The covenant threshing floor of Ornan becomes the covenant site of the covenant temple — Chapter 22:1. The covenant covenant of the covenant temple is a covenant consequence of the covenant crisis of the covenant census.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 13 — let me fall into the hand of YHWH, for his mercy is very great. Dawid's covenant choice of YHWH's covenant judgment over human covenant judgment is the covenant expression of covenant trust in YHWH's covenant character. Dawid knows YHWH's covenant mercy even in covenant judgment better than he knows human covenant mercy. The covenant choice is covenant theologically revealing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 24 — I will not offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing. The covenant principle of costly covenant worship: the covenant king refuses to worship YHWH with offerings that cost him nothing. Covenant genuine covenant worship requires covenant genuine covenant sacrifice — the covenant offering that costs the covenant worshiper nothing is not a covenant worthy covenant offering.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — Satan stood against Yisra'EL and incited Dawid to number Yisra'EL. The covenant parallel in Shemu'el Bet 24:1 attributes the incitement to YHWH — the covenant theological tension between covenant divine sovereignty and covenant creaturely covenant agency. The covenant Divrei HaYamim version introduces the covenant adversarial figure explicitly. Both covenant texts are covenant theologically consistent with the covenant understanding that YHWH permits what he does not approve of.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 22 This Is the House of YHWH Creator — Dawid Prepared Materials for the Temple — You Are a Man of War — Your Son Shelomoh Shall Build the House — Be Strong and Courageous

1 Then Dawid said, Here shall be the house of YHWH Creator and here the altar of burnt offering for Yisra'EL.

2 Dawid commanded to gather together the sojourners who were in the land of Yisra'EL, and he set stonecutters to cut dressed stones for building the house of Creator.

3 Dawid also provided great quantities of iron for nails for the doors of the gates and for clamps, as well as bronze in quantities beyond weighing,

4 And cedar timbers without number, for the Sidonians and Tyrians brought great quantities of cedar to Dawid.

5 For Dawid said, Shelomoh my son is young and inexperienced, and the house that is to be built for YHWH must be exceedingly magnificent, of fame and glory throughout all lands. I will therefore make preparation for it. So Dawid made ample preparations before his death.

6 Then he called for Shelomoh his son and charged him to build a house for YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

7 Dawid said to Shelomoh, My son, I had it in my heart to build a house to the name of YHWH my Creator.

8 But the word of YHWH came to me, saying, You have shed much blood and have waged great wars. You shall not build a house to my name, because you have shed so much blood before me on the earth.

9 Behold, a son shall be born to you who shall be a man of rest. I will give him rest from all his surrounding enemies. For his name shall be Shelomoh, and I will give peace and quiet to Yisra'EL in his days.

10 He shall build a house for my name. He shall be my son, and I will be his father, and I will establish his royal throne over Yisra'EL forever.

11 Now, my son, YHWH be with you, so that you may succeed in building the house of YHWH your Creator, as he has spoken concerning you.

12 Only, may YHWH grant you discretion and understanding, that when he gives you charge over Yisra'EL you may keep the Torah of YHWH your Creator.

13 Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the rules that YHWH commanded Mosheh for Yisra'EL. Be strong and courageous. Fear not. Do not be dismayed.

14 With great pains I have provided for the house of YHWH 100,000 talents of gold, 1,000,000 talents of silver, and bronze and iron beyond weighing, for there is so much of it. Timber and stone too I have provided. To these you must add more.

15 You have an abundance of workmen: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and all kinds of craftsmen without number, skilled in working

16 Gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Arise and work. YHWH be with you.

17 Dawid also commanded all the leaders of Yisra'EL to help Shelomoh his son, saying,

18 Is not YHWH your Creator with you? And has he not given you peace on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hand, and the land is subdued before YHWH and his people.

19 Now set your mind and heart to seek YHWH your Creator. Arise and build the sanctuary of YHWH Creator, so that the ark of the covenant of YHWH and the holy vessels of Creator may be brought into a house built for the name of YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 22 is the covenant preparation chapter: Dawid's covenant massive covenant provision of covenant materials — gold, silver, bronze, iron, cedar, stone — for the covenant temple that Shelomoh will build. The covenant reason Dawid cannot build — he is a covenant man of war and has shed blood — is given explicitly for the first time in verse 8. The covenant builder must be a covenant man of peace — Shelomoh, whose name means peace.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verse 12-13 — may YHWH grant you discretion and understanding, that you may keep the Torah of YHWH your Creator. Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the rules that YHWH commanded Mosheh. Be strong and courageous. Dawid's covenant charge to Shelomoh: the covenant prosperity of the covenant temple builder depends on covenant faithful covenant Torah observance, not on covenant political covenant wisdom or covenant military covenant power.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verse 14 — with great pains I have provided 100,000 talents of gold, 1,000,000 talents of silver. The covenant scale of Dawid's covenant provision is covenant hyperbolic in its covenant grandeur — the covenant compiler communicates the covenant magnitude of the covenant preparation through covenant numerical covenant overstatement. The covenant temple deserves the covenant community's covenant maximum covenant investment.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — the house that is to be built for YHWH must be exceedingly magnificent, of fame and glory throughout all lands. Dawid's covenant motivation for covenant massive covenant preparation: the covenant house of YHWH must exceed all other covenant human covenant constructions in its covenant magnificence. The covenant covenant standard for covenant divine covenant worship is covenant superlative — the covenant best available, not the covenant convenient or the covenant expedient.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 23 Dawid Made Shelomoh King — He Numbered the Levites — The Sons of Gershon — The Sons of Kohath — The Sons of Merari — The Duties of the Levites Changed

1 When Dawid was old and full of days, he made Shelomoh his son king over Yisra'EL.

2 Dawid assembled all the leaders of Yisra'EL and the priests and the Levites.

3 The Levites, thirty years old and upward, were numbered, and the total was 38,000 men.

4 Twenty-four thousand of these, said Dawid, shall have oversight of the work in the house of YHWH, 6,000 shall be officers and judges,

5 4,000 gatekeepers, and 4,000 shall offer praises to YHWH with the instruments that I have made for praise.

6 And Dawid organized them in divisions corresponding to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

7 The sons of Gershon: La'dan and Shim'i.

8 The sons of La'dan: Yekhiel the chief, and Zetam and Yo'el, three.

9 The sons of Shim'i: Shelomit, Hazi'el, and Haran, three. These were the heads of the fathers' houses of La'dan.

10 And the sons of Shim'i: Yakhat, Zina, and Ye'ush and Beri'ah. These four were the sons of Shim'i.

11 Yakhat was the chief, and Zizah the second. But Ye'ush and Beri'ah did not have many sons, so they became a single fathers' house.

12 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Yitzhar, Khevron, and Uzzi'el, four.

13 The sons of Amram: Aharon and Mosheh. Aharon was set apart to dedicate the most holy things, that he and his sons forever should make offerings before YHWH, minister to him and pronounce blessings in his name forever.

14 But the sons of Mosheh the man of Creator were named among the tribe of Levi.

15 The sons of Mosheh: Gershom and Eli'ezer.

16 The sons of Gershom: Shevuel the chief.

17 The sons of Eli'ezer: Rekhavyahu the chief. Eli'ezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rekhavyahu were very many.

18 The sons of Yitzhar: Shelomit the chief.

19 The sons of Khevron: Yeriyahu the chief, Amaryahu the second, Yakhazi'el the third, and Yekame'am the fourth.

20 The sons of Uzzi'el: Mikha the chief and Yishshiyahu the second.

21 The sons of Merari: Makhli and Mushi. The sons of Makhli: El'azar and Kish.

22 El'azar died having no sons, but only daughters. Their kinsmen, the sons of Kish, married them.

23 The sons of Mushi: Makhli, Eder, and Yeremot, three.

24 These were the sons of Levi by their fathers' houses, the heads of the fathers' houses as they were listed according to the number of the names of the individuals from twenty years old and upward who did the work for the service of the house of YHWH.

25 For Dawid said, YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, has given rest to his people, and he dwells in Yerushalayim forever.

26 And so the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the things for its service.

27 For by the last words of Dawid the sons of Levi were numbered from twenty years old and upward.

28 For their duty was to assist the sons of Aharon for service in the house of YHWH, having the care of the courts and the chambers, the cleansing of all that is holy, and any work for the service of the house of Creator.

29 Their duty was also to assist with the showbread, the flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baked offering, the offering mixed with oil, and all measures of quantity and size.

30 And they were to stand every morning, thanking and praising YHWH, and likewise at evening,

31 And whenever burnt offerings were offered to YHWH on Shabbatot, new moons, and feast days, according to the number required of them, regularly before YHWH.

32 Thus they were to keep charge of the tent of meeting and the sanctuary, and to attend the sons of Aharon, their brothers, for the service of the house of YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 23 establishes the covenant Levitical organization for the covenant temple service — the covenant administrative framework that Dawid prepares even though he cannot build the covenant temple itself. The covenant age of service is changed from thirty years — Bemidbar 4:3 — to twenty years — verse 27 — because the covenant carrying of the covenant ark is no longer required and the covenant Levitical service now takes a covenant different covenant form.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 25-26 — YHWH has given rest to his people and he dwells in Yerushalayim forever. And so the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle. The covenant theological significance of the covenant settlement: the covenant period of covenant wilderness covenant mobility is over. YHWH dwelling in Yerushalayim permanently changes the nature of the covenant Levitical covenant service from covenant porterage to covenant permanent covenant ministry.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 13 — Aharon was set apart to dedicate the most holy things, that he and his sons forever should make offerings before YHWH, minister to him and pronounce blessings in his name forever. The covenant Aaronide covenant priestly covenant distinctives are specified: the most holy things, the covenant offerings, the covenant ministry before YHWH, and the covenant pronouncement of covenant blessings. These are covenant specifically Aaronide covenant functions within the broader covenant Levitical covenant service.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant organizational work of Chapters 23-27 is the covenant most distinctive contribution of Divrei HaYamim to the Dawid narrative: the covenant compiler presents Dawid as the covenant organizer of the covenant temple covenant institutional structure as much as the covenant military conqueror who secured the covenant land. Dawid's covenant greatest covenant legacy, in the covenant compiler's covenant vision, is the covenant institutional preparation for covenant worship.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 24 The Divisions of the Sons of Aharon — Tzadok and Akhimelekh — They Were Divided by Lot — The Twenty-Four Divisions of the Priests — The Rest of the Levites

1 The divisions of the sons of Aharon were these. The sons of Aharon: Nadav, Avihu, El'azar, and Itamar.

2 But Nadav and Avihu died before their father and had no children, so El'azar and Itamar became the priests.

8 The third to Harim, the fourth to Se'orim,

9 The fifth to Malkiyah, the sixth to Miyamin,

10 The seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Aviyah,

11 The ninth to Yeshua, the tenth to Shekhanyahu,

12 The eleventh to Elyashiv, the twelfth to Yakim,

13 The thirteenth to Khuppah, the fourteenth to Yeshevav,

14 The fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 24 establishes the covenant twenty-four divisions of the covenant priestly service — the covenant institutional framework that will govern covenant temple covenant worship for centuries. The covenant lot-casting — verse 5, 31 — is the covenant democratic covenant mechanism for assigning covenant service periods without covenant favoritism: the covenant divine determination of covenant practical covenant organization.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 5 — they divided them by lot, all alike, for there were sacred officers and officers of Creator among both the sons of El'azar and the sons of Itamar. The covenant equality of the covenant lot: the covenant priestly families of both El'azar and Itamar are treated with the same covenant dignity in the covenant lot-casting. The covenant organizational process treats both covenant priestly lines with covenant equal covenant respect.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 19 — these had as their appointed duty in their service to come into the house of YHWH according to the procedure established for them by Aharon their father, as YHWH Creator had commanded him. The covenant twenty-four divisions are grounded in the covenant Aaronide covenant commission: the covenant institutional organization is not a covenant Davidic covenant innovation but the covenant fulfillment of the covenant foundational covenant instruction given to Aharon.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant twenty-four priestly divisions established in Chapter 24 are the covenant organizational foundation that survived the Babylonian exile and returned with the covenant post-exilic community — as Ezra 2 and Nekhemyah 12 document. The covenant persistence of the covenant twenty-four divisions through the covenant exile demonstrates the covenant resilience of the covenant institutional framework that Dawid established: the covenant organization outlasted the covenant political covenant structures it served.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 25 establishes the twenty-four divisions of the covenant Levitical singers — precisely paralleling the twenty-four divisions of the covenant priests in Chapter 24.
