21 The sons of La'dan, the sons of the Gershonites belonging to La'dan, the heads of the fathers' houses of La'dan the Gershonite: Yekhi'eli.

22 The sons of Yekhi'eli, Zetam, and Yo'el his brother, were in charge of the treasuries of the house of YHWH.

23 Of the Amramites, the Yitzharites, the Khevronites, and the Uzzi'elites —

24 And Shevuel the son of Gershom, son of Mosheh, was chief officer in charge of the treasuries.

25 His brothers: from Eli'ezer were his son Rekhavyahu, and his son Yeshayahu, and his son Yoram, and his son Zikhri, and his son Shelomot.

26 This Shelomot and his brothers were in charge of all the treasuries of the dedicated gifts that Dawid the king and the heads of the fathers' houses and the officers of the thousands and the hundreds and the commanders of the army had dedicated.

27 From spoil won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of YHWH.

28 Also all that Shemu'el the seer and Sha'ul the son of Kish and Avner the son of Ner and Yo'av the son of Tzeruyah had dedicated — all dedicated gifts were in the care of Shelomot and his brothers.

29 Of the Yitzharites, Kenanyahu and his sons were appointed to outside duties for Yisra'EL, as officers and judges.

30 Of the Khevronites, Khashabyahu and his brothers, 1,700 men of ability, had the oversight of Yisra'EL westward of the Yarden for all the work of YHWH and for the service of the king.

31 Of the Khevronites, Yeriyah was chief of the Khevronites of whatever genealogy or fathers' houses. In the fortieth year of Dawid's reign search was made and men of great ability among them were found at Yazer of Gil'ad.

32 King Dawid appointed him and his brothers, 2,700 men of ability, heads of fathers' houses, to have the oversight of the Re'uvenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Menasheh for everything pertaining to Creator and for the affairs of the king.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 26 establishes the covenant gatekeeper divisions and the covenant treasury oversight — two more covenant institutional covenant pillars of the covenant temple service. The covenant gatekeepers are not merely covenant doormen but covenant security and covenant administrative officers whose covenant presence maintains the covenant integrity of the covenant sacred spaces.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full. Verse 27-28 — from spoil won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of YHWH — including what Shemu'el and Sha'ul and Avner and Yo'av had dedicated. The covenant treasury of dedicated covenant gifts spans multiple generations and multiple covenant leaders: the covenant community's covenant dedication to YHWH's covenant house has a covenant multigenerational covenant history.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 26 in full. Verse 13 — they cast lots by fathers' houses, small and great alike, for their gates. The covenant egalitarian covenant lot-casting extends to the covenant gatekeepers: no covenant gatekeeper family receives privileged covenant assignment through covenant human covenant favoritism — the covenant divine covenant lot determines the covenant posting. Covenant impartiality is the covenant organizational covenant principle.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant Oved-Edom of the covenant gatekeeper families — verses 4-8 — is the same Oved-Edom whose household was blessed when the covenant ark dwelt there — Chapter 13:14. The covenant man who received the covenant ark into his household now has his covenant descendants serving as covenant gatekeepers of the covenant temple. The covenant blessing of covenant hospitality to YHWH's covenant presence translates into covenant institutional covenant service in the covenant temple.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 27 The Twelve Monthly Divisions of the Army — The Twelve Commanders — The Tribal Leaders — The Overseers of the King's Properties — The Advisors of Dawid

1 This is the list of the people of Yisra'EL, the heads of fathers' houses, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who served the king in all matters concerning the divisions that came and went, month after month throughout the year, each division numbering 24,000:

2 Yashov'am the son of Zavdi'el was in charge of the first division in the first month. In his division were 24,000.

3 He was a descendant of Peretz and was chief of all the commanders. He served in the first month.

4 Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division of the second month. Miklot was the chief officer of his division. In his division were 24,000.

5 The third commander, for the third month, was Benayahu the son of Yehoyada the chief priest. In his division were 24,000.

6 This is the Benayahu who was a mighty man of the thirty and in command of the thirty. His son Ammizavad was in charge of his division.

7 Asahel the brother of Yo'av was fourth, for the fourth month, and his son Zevadyahu after him. In his division were 24,000.

8 The fifth commander, for the fifth month, was Shamhut the Yizrahite. In his division were 24,000.

9 Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite. In his division were 24,000.

10 Seventh, for the seventh month, was Kheletz the Pelonite, of the sons of Efrayim. In his division were 24,000.

11 Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites. In his division were 24,000.

12 Ninth, for the ninth month, was Avi'ezer the Antathite, of the Binyaminites. In his division were 24,000.

13 Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netofathite, of the Zerahites. In his division were 24,000.

14 Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benayahu the Piratonite, of the sons of Efrayim. In his division were 24,000.

15 Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Kheldai the Netofathite, of Otni'el. In his division were 24,000.

16 Over the tribes of Yisra'EL: for the Re'uvenites, Eliezer the son of Zikhri was chief officer. For the Shim'onites, Shefatyahu the son of Ma'akhah.

17 For Levi, Khashabyahu the son of Kemu'el. For Aharon, Tzadok.

18 For Yehudah, Elihu, one of Dawid's brothers. For Yissachar, Omri the son of Mikha'el.

19 For Zevulun, Yishma'yahu the son of Ovadyahu. For Naftali, Yerimot the son of Azri'el.

20 For the sons of Efrayim, Hoshe'a the son of Azazyahu. For the half-tribe of Menasheh, Yo'el the son of Pedayahu.

21 For the half of Menasheh in Gil'ad, Iddo the son of Zekhariyahu. For Binyamin, Ya'asi'el the son of Avner.

22 For Dan, Azar'el the son of Yerokham. These were the commanders of the tribes of Yisra'EL.

23 Dawid did not number those below twenty years of age, for YHWH had promised to make Yisra'EL as many as the stars of heaven.

24 Yo'av the son of Tzeruyah began to count, but did not finish. Yet wrath came upon Yisra'EL for this, and the number was not entered in the chronicles of King Dawid.

25 Over the king's treasuries was Azmaveth the son of Adi'el. Over the treasuries in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers was Yehonatan the son of Uziyahu.

26 Over those who did the work of the field, tilling the soil, was Ezri the son of Kelub.

27 Over the vineyards was Shim'i the Ramathite. Over the produce of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zavdi the Shafamite.

28 Over the olive trees and the sycamore trees in the Shephelah was Ba'al-Khanan the Gederite. Over the stores of oil was Yo'ash.

29 Over the herds that pastured in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite. Over the herds in the valleys was Shafat the son of Adlai.

30 Over the camels was Ovil the Yishmaelite. Over the donkeys was Yekhdeiyahu the Meronothite.

31 Over the flocks was Yaziz the Hagarite. All these were stewards of King Dawid's property.

32 Yonatan, Dawid's uncle, was a counselor, being a man of understanding and a scribe. He and Yekhiel the son of Khakhmoni attended the king's sons.

33 Akhitofel was the king's counselor. Khushai the Arkhite was the king's friend.

34 After Akhitofel came Yehoyada the son of Benayahu, and Evyatar. Yo'av was the commander of the king's army.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 27 completes the covenant administrative organization of the covenant Davidic state: twelve covenant monthly divisions of the army — 24,000 per month, 288,000 per year — covenant tribal covenant leaders, covenant property managers, and covenant royal covenant advisors. The covenant Davidic kingdom in Divrei HaYamim is the covenant most thoroughly organized covenant state in the covenant historical narrative.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full. Verse 23-24 — Dawid did not number those below twenty years of age, for YHWH had promised to make Yisra'EL as many as the stars of heaven. Yo'av began to count but did not finish, and wrath came upon Yisra'EL. The covenant connection to Chapter 21: the covenant unlawful census that produced the covenant plague is now explained more fully — the covenant counting was incomplete and covenant unauthorized for those under twenty.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 27 in full. The covenant administrative detail of verses 25-31 — who manages the king's covenant agricultural covenant properties, his covenant herds, his covenant vineyards, his covenant camels — presents Dawid as a covenant complete covenant ruler who administers not only covenant military and covenant religious affairs but covenant economic covenant governance as well.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 33 — Akhitofel was the king's counselor. The covenant inclusion of Akhitofel — the covenant counselor who later sided with Avshalom against Dawid — within the covenant official list of the covenant Davidic administration is the covenant honest covenant record that does not sanitize the covenant Davidic court. The covenant administration included men who would eventually betray the covenant king.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 28 Dawid Assembled All the Leaders — The Creator Chose Shelomoh — Be Strong and Courageous and Do It — Dawid Gave Shelomoh the Plan — The Plan by the Spirit — Everything for the Service

1 Dawid assembled at Yerushalayim all the officials of Yisra'EL, the officials of the tribes, the commanders of the divisions that served the king, the commanders of thousands, the commanders of hundreds, the stewards of all the property and livestock of the king and his sons, together with the palace officials, the mighty men and all the seasoned warriors.

2 Then King Dawid rose to his feet and said, Hear me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of YHWH and for the footstool of our Creator, and I made preparations for building.

3 But Creator said to me, You may not build a house for my name, for you are a man of war and have shed blood.

4 Yet YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL chose me from all my father's house to be king over Yisra'EL forever. For he chose Yehudah as leader, and in the house of Yehudah my father's house, and among my father's sons he took pleasure in me to make me king over all Yisra'EL.

5 And of all my sons — for YHWH has given me many sons — he has chosen Shelomoh my son to sit on the throne of the kingdom of YHWH over Yisra'EL.

6 He said to me, It is Shelomoh your son who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.

7 I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues steadfast in keeping my commandments and my rules, as he is today.

8 Now therefore in the sight of all Yisra'EL, the assembly of YHWH, and in the hearing of our Creator, observe and seek out all the commandments of YHWH your Creator, that you may possess this good land and leave it for an inheritance to your children after you forever.

9 And you, Shelomoh my son, know the Creator of your father and serve him with a whole heart and with a willing mind, for YHWH searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever.

10 Be careful now, for YHWH has chosen you to build a house for the sanctuary. Be strong and do it.

11 Then Dawid gave Shelomoh his son the plan of the vestibule of the temple, and of its houses, its treasuries, its upper rooms, and its inner chambers, and of the room for the mercy seat.

12 And the plan of all that he had in mind for the courts of the house of YHWH, all the surrounding chambers, the treasuries of the house of Creator, and the treasuries for dedicated gifts.

13 For the divisions of the priests and of the Levites, and all the work of the service in the house of YHWH, for all the vessels for the service in the house of YHWH,

14 The weight of gold for all golden vessels for each service, the weight of silver vessels for each service,

15 The weight of the golden lampstands and their lamps, the weight of gold for each lampstand and its lamps, the weight of silver for a lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each lampstand in the service,

16 The weight of gold for each table for the showbread, the weight of silver for the silver tables,

17 And pure gold for the forks, the basins, and the cups, the weight of gold for each golden bowl, the weight of silver for each silver bowl,

18 The weight of refined gold for the altar of incense, and the plan of the golden chariot of the cherubim that spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of YHWH.

19 All this he made clear to me in writing from the hand of YHWH, all the work to be done according to the plan.

20 Then Dawid said to Shelomoh his son, Be strong and courageous and do it. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed, for YHWH Creator, my Creator, is with you. He will not leave you or forsake you, until all the work for the service of the house of YHWH is finished.

21 And here are the divisions of the priests and the Levites for all the service of the house of Creator. There will be with you for all the work, for all the service, every willing man who has skill for any kind of service. Also the officers and all the people will be wholly at your command.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 28 is the covenant great assembly address of Dawid to the covenant national covenant leadership — his last major covenant public covenant speech. The covenant theological heart of the speech is verse 9: know the Creator of your father and serve him with a whole heart and a willing mind, for YHWH searches all hearts and understands every plan and thought. The covenant inner life is the covenant foundation of covenant covenant-keeping.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 19 — all this he made clear to me in writing from the hand of YHWH, all the work to be done according to the plan. The covenant divine revelation of the covenant temple plan: Dawid claims that the covenant architectural plan of the covenant temple was covenant revealed to him by YHWH — making the covenant temple plan covenant parallel to the covenant tabernacle plan revealed to Mosheh in Shemot 25-30.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 20 — be strong and courageous and do it. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed, for YHWH Creator, my Creator, is with you. He will not leave you or forsake you. The covenant language is the same covenant formula used in Devarim 31 — Mosheh charging Yehoshua before the covenant entry into the covenant land. Dawid charges Shelomoh with the covenant formula of covenant transition from the covenant predecessor.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues steadfast in keeping my commandments and my rules, as he is today. The covenant conditional element of the covenant Davidic covenant: the covenant eternal covenant promise of the Davidic dynasty carries a covenant practical covenant condition — covenant faithful covenant Torah observance. The covenant eternity of the covenant promise does not eliminate the covenant personal covenant accountability of the covenant king who sits on the covenant throne.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 29 Who Will Offer Willingly — The Leaders Gave Willingly — Blessed Are You YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL Our Father — Everything Comes From You — All This Abundance We Have Provided From Your Hand — They Made Shelomoh King — Dawid Died in a Good Old Age

1 And King Dawid said to all the assembly, Shelomoh my son, whom alone Creator has chosen, is young and inexperienced, and the work is great, for the palace will not be for man but for YHWH Creator.

2 So I have provided for the house of my Creator, so far as I was able, the gold for the things of gold, the silver for the things of silver, and the bronze for the things of bronze, the iron for the things of iron, and wood for the things of wood, besides great quantities of onyx and stones for setting, antimony, colored stones, all sorts of precious stones, and marble.

3 Moreover, in addition to all that I have provided for the holy house, I have a treasure of my own of gold and silver, and because of my devotion to the house of my Creator I give it to the house of my Creator:

4 Three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ofir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the house.

5 The gold for the things of gold and the silver for the things of silver and for all the work to be done by craftsmen. Who then will offer willingly, consecrating himself today to YHWH?

6 Then the leaders of fathers' houses made their freewill offerings, as did also the leaders of the tribes, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the officers over the king's work.

7 They gave for the service of the house of Creator 5,000 talents and 10,000 darics of gold, 10,000 talents of silver, 18,000 talents of bronze, and 100,000 talents of iron.

8 And whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of YHWH, in the care of Yekhiel the Gershonite.

9 Then the people rejoiced because they had given willingly, for with a whole heart they had offered freely to YHWH. Dawid the king also rejoiced greatly.

10 Therefore Dawid blessed YHWH in the presence of all the assembly. And Dawid said, Blessed are you, O YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL our father, forever and ever.

11 Yours, O YHWH, is the greatness and the power and the glory and the victory and the majesty, for all that is in the heavens and in the earth is yours. Yours is the kingdom, O YHWH, and you are exalted as head above all.

12 Both riches and honor come from you, and you rule over all. In your hand are power and might, and in your hand it is to make great and to give strength to all.

13 And now we thank you, our Creator, and praise your glorious name.

14 But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able thus to offer willingly? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you.

15 For we are strangers before you and sojourners, as all our fathers were. Our days on the earth are like a shadow, and there is no abiding.

16 O YHWH our Creator, all this abundance that we have provided for building you a house for your holy name comes from your hand and is all your own.

17 I know, my Creator, that you test the heart and have pleasure in uprightness. In the uprightness of my heart I have freely offered all these things, and now I have seen your people, who are present here, offering freely and joyously to you.

18 O YHWH, the Creator of Avraham, Yitzkhak, and Yisra'EL, our fathers, keep forever such purposes and thoughts in the hearts of your people, and direct their hearts toward you.

19 Grant to Shelomoh my son a whole heart that he may keep your commandments, your testimonies, and your statutes, performing all, and that he may build the palace for which I have made provision.

20 Then Dawid said to all the assembly, Bless YHWH your Creator. And all the assembly blessed YHWH, the Creator of their fathers, and bowed their heads and paid homage to YHWH and to the king.

21 And they offered sacrifices to YHWH, and on the next day offered burnt offerings to YHWH, 1,000 bulls, 1,000 rams, and 1,000 lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Yisra'EL.

22 And they ate and drank before YHWH on that day with great gladness. And they made Shelomoh the son of Dawid king the second time, and anointed him as prince for YHWH, and Tzadok as priest.

23 Then Shelomoh sat on the throne of YHWH as king in place of Dawid his father. And he prospered, and all Yisra'EL obeyed him.

24 All the leaders and the mighty men, and also all the sons of King Dawid, pledged their allegiance to King Shelomoh.

25 And YHWH made Shelomoh very great in the sight of all Yisra'EL and bestowed on him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Yisra'EL.

26 Thus Dawid the son of Yishai reigned over all Yisra'EL.

27 The time that he reigned over Yisra'EL was forty years. He reigned seven years in Khevron and thirty-three years in Yerushalayim.

28 Then he died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honor. And Shelomoh his son reigned in his place.

29 Now the acts of King Dawid, from first to last, are written in the Chronicles of Shemu'el the seer, and in the Chronicles of Natan the prophet, and in the Chronicles of Gad the seer,

30 With accounts of all his rule and his might and of the circumstances that came upon him and upon Yisra'EL and upon all the kingdoms of the countries.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 29 is the covenant conclusion of the Dawid narrative — the covenant great freewill offering for the covenant temple, Dawid's covenant prayer of covenant praise, and Dawid's covenant death in covenant fullness. The covenant prayer of verse 10-19 is the covenant theological summit of the book: Blessed are you YHWH — all that is in heaven and earth is yours — from your hand have we given you.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 14-15 — who am I and what is my people, that we should be able to offer willingly? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you. For we are strangers before you and sojourners. The covenant theology of covenant stewardship: the covenant community can only return to YHWH what YHWH first gave to them. The covenant wealth consecrated to the covenant temple was YHWH's before it was Dawid's.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 28 — Dawid died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honor. The covenant epitaph of the covenant greatest king of Yisra'EL: full of days — covenant fulfilled covenant life — riches — covenant material covenant blessing — and honor — covenant communal covenant recognition. The covenant death is covenant complete: nothing left covenant unfinished, nothing covenant lacking.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — the people rejoiced because they had given willingly, for with a whole heart they had offered freely to YHWH. The covenant character of the covenant offering determines the covenant character of the covenant joy: it is the covenant willingness — the covenant whole-heartedness — of the covenant offering that produces the covenant joy, not merely the covenant quantity of the covenant gift. Covenant joyful covenant worship and covenant joyful covenant generosity are covenant inseparable in the covenant compiler's covenant theological vision.
